what_it_does: It compiles a round-by-round inventory of existing code, services, schemas, and design agreements for a channel-memory plan, marks each item's verification status, and derives planning implications so downstream criteria and guides are grounded rather than aspirational.
observations: Organized into verification rounds (1–7, with 5 pending) and a metadata header (trust, author, date, verified status).
Round 1 inventories runtime/session_scan.py, session_recall.py, session_lens.py, and flags session_search.py as broken/deprecated.
Round 2 verifies live services on :8007 (pplx-embed) and :8008 (jina rerank) and notes an existing substrate index.
Round 3 documents the session-store JSONL grammar, boundary detection, and lineage mapping.
Round 4 confirms runtime/cognition.py role/cascade capabilities and resolves a caveat about the json_schema generation policy.
Round 5 is pending lead-lane exploration of channels, source registry, and rule-load mechanisms.
Round 7 (before Round 6) records agreement on one layered memory system: session-recall inside recollection, no parallel systems.
Round 6 identifies the feedback-*.md preference-memory corpus as the get-a-feel source.
Includes explicit workflow: new findings → this file → Guide → Criteria.
Notes jina-v3 reranker is CC-BY-NC, requiring a permissive swap for commercial use.
standouts: session_search.py is deprecated and must not be built on.
The synthesis panel should build on runtime/cognition.py roles using the existing json_schema policy; no engine fix is needed.
One layered memory system is agreed: session-recall is the inner layer, recollection is the outer layer; no parallel memory system.
Round 5 (channels/registry/rule-load) and part of Round 4 remain pending on the lead lane.
Preference/identity layer is recollection's Pillar-1, fed by the feedback-* corpus.
conventions: Uses verification tags like [V built], [V verified live], [V read], [PENDING].
Applies principle 2: do not build parallel systems; check existing equivalents first.
References people/roles with shorthand (lead, Tim, fork).
Breaks sequential round ordering by placing Round 7 before Round 6.
concerns: Several planning decisions depend on pending lead-lane verification (Round 5 and part of Round 4).
Round numbering is out of order, which could confuse readers.
Heavy use of project-specific shorthand and cross-references makes the file opaque without surrounding context.
Commercial licensing risk for the jina-v3 reranker is noted but not resolved.
Some claims rely on external verification status rather than content inside this file.
notes: This is the evidence-base ledger: it tells the plan what already exists, what is deprecated, and what still needs lead-lane verification.
questions: What is the exact content and schema of the 'Criteria' and 'Guide' documents this file feeds?
What is the structure of the source registry and channel membership/load mechanism mentioned in Round 5?
What are the concrete output schemas and prompt templates for the proposed preference_extract and facet-extract roles?
What does 'lattice-with-hierarchy-as-axes' mean operationally for addressing and recall?
What is the current verification status of Round 4 'one caveat' and Round 5 items?
What do 'Group 4', 'Group 5/6', and 'Group 3.7' refer to in the implications section?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is a synthesis of existing evidence and uses that to direct build decisions.
apparent_themes: Cross-session preference/identity as a first-class pillar
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent parallel or duplicate builds by making the existing substrate visible, unblock the synthesis-panel path by confirming the json_schema policy, and position session-recall as a nested inner layer within a single recollection memory system.
novelty: It treats research findings as a versioned, verification-tagged ledger with an explicit flow into downstream plan documents, and it records a resolved caveat about an engine policy rather than leaving it as a blocker.
contribution: A structured, status-tagged inventory of existing runtime, services, cognition, and memory assets plus explicit anti-duplication guidance that a fused plan can use to route new work through verified capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A research synthesis markdown file that inventories verified and pending existing substrates for a channel-memory plan, flags deprecated components, and derives build implications.
intention_for_embedding: To ground planning criteria and guides in an evidence base so new builds reuse existing runtime, cognition, and memory substrates instead of creating parallel systems.
